message:
feat(ipc): tick 192 run_with_context_with_cx accept-poll uses timeout_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

IpcServer::run_with_context_with_cx was using ambient
`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(IPC_ACCEPT_POLL_INTERVAL, ...)` for
its accept poll. The ambient `timeout` helper falls back to
`Cx::current()` thread-local lookup (runtime_compat.rs L2163) — if
the server loop runs outside a thread-local cx scope (e.g. spawned
directly from CLI startup, or entered from a context that didn't
install the caller's cx), the accept poll runs under a fresh
request-scoped cx instead of the caller's.

Tick 192 routes the accept poll through
`crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx(cx, ...)` so the caller's
explicit cx bounds the poll. Matches the pattern already used by
native_events::run_with_cx (native_events.rs L291).

This closes the last orphan-cx hole in the IPC server's cx-first
entry point. The per-connection spawn already clones cx into the
child task (L958) and hands it to handle_client_with_context_with_cx.

Legacy run_with_context (L875) is unchanged.

Verification: 98 / 98 ipc:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including
ipc_server_run_with_cx_exits_cleanly_on_shutdown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -950,8 +950,19 @@ impl IpcServer {
                 break;
             }
 
-            match crate::runtime_compat::timeout(IPC_ACCEPT_POLL_INTERVAL, self.listener.accept())
-                .await
+            // Tick 192 (ft-xbnl0.2.3): accept-poll now bounded by the
+            // caller's cx via timeout_with_cx, matching the pattern
+            // used by native_events::run_with_cx. Previously this
+            // used ambient `timeout` which falls back to
+            // `Cx::current()` thread-local lookup — orphan cx hole
+            // whenever the server loop runs outside a thread-local
+            // cx scope (e.g. spawned directly from CLI startup).
+            match crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx(
+                cx,
+                IPC_ACCEPT_POLL_INTERVAL,
+                self.listener.accept(),
+            )
+            .await
             {
                 Ok(Ok((stream, _addr))) => {
                     let ctx = ctx.clone();
